title: Chazaliella gossweileri
intro: Chazaliella gossweileri is a medicinal plant species, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and malaria, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Chazaliella gossweileri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made with Chazaliella gossweileri include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat fever, pain, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Chazaliella gossweileri is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant is native to Angola. It belongs to the genus Chazaliella, which is a part of the Rubiaceae family. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Chazaliella gossweileri is classified as an evergreen shrub.
